items.headline: Police: Stalking suspect fired at Moline PD during arrest, hit squad car
items.summary: A stalking suspect is accused of firing at a Moline police vehicle while fleeing officers on Wednesday. No injuries were reported.

items.headline: Silvis Ethics Commission holds hearing following alderman's resignation
items.summary: Former Alderman Craig Pirmann was accused of recording a closed-door City Council meeting.

items.headline: Pritzker says he doesn’t know details of deadly Jan. 4 shooting by Illinois State Police
items.summary: The governor refers to the unarmed mother who was shot as a "victim," but says he’s otherwise not ready to comment.

items.headline: Goose Creek Spray Pad closed due to infrastructure, mechanical issues
items.summary: Residents who cool off at the Goose Creek Spray Pad in Davenport will have to find other ways to beat the heat. A post on the Davenport Parks & Recreation’s…

items.headline: Pay It Forward: Pam's Place supporting Quad Cities
items.summary: Pamela Seales was presented with the Pay It Forward award for her hard work and dedication in fostering a strong and resourceful community.

items.headline: Regular customer charged in 2025 Davenport vape shop robbery
items.summary: A Davenport man is charged with first-degree robbery after police say he threatened a vape shop employee with a knife during a 2025 robbery.

items.headline: Quad Cities Community Foundation launches nonprofit networking series
items.summary: The Quad Cities Community Foundation has a new way to help nonprofits find skilled, engaged board members. The foundation is launching Quad Cities Board Connect…

items.headline: Have you seen these suspects? Crime Stoppers wants to know!
items.summary: Crime Stoppers of the Quad Cities wants your help catching two fugitives.  It’s an Our Quad Cities News exclusive. You can get an elevated reward for informatio…

items.headline: Multiple departments respond to Carbon Cliff fire
items.summary: UPDATE: The Our Quad Cities news crew has received more information on the fire in Carbon Cliff. The initial call went out at 9:34 a.m. Silvis Fire Department a…

items.headline: New Iowa law eliminates racial and cultural awareness, bias prevention training requirements. How local police, NAACP respond
items.summary: Some local departments say they're going to continue the trainings, but critics worry the new law sets the state back in terms of criminal justice reform.

items.headline: Illinois man speaks for the first time in 12 years thanks to AI program
items.summary: "We're able to have a working voice that's based on his vocal data,” Cody Pees said. "Him hearing his own voice again was an experience you can’t even imagine.”

items.headline: MercyOne centers honored for treatment of heart disease, stroke
